supporting_evidence: State Tier**: Mandates *geographically calibrated pedagogy*, where state-specific histories (e.g., plantation capitalism in the South, frontier labor in the West, industrial conflict in the Northeast) inform curriculum design and public memory initiatives.

County Tier**: Requires *layered implementation*—urban districts emphasize global labor networks and immigrant labor movements; suburban districts focus on class formation and ideology; rural districts center agrarian materialism (e.g., Populism, tenant farming, resource extraction).
